patterns.name: Small Writing Slips
patterns.what: Sometimes loses marks on written answers due to small spelling mistakes, forgetting to capitalise 'I' or names, or accidentally adding extra words.

specific_examples.whatWentWrong: EG forgot to capitalise the letter 'I' in the middle of the sentence.
EG added an extra 'd' when spelling 'sadness'.
EG accidentally mixed up the letters when spelling 'doubt'.

patterns.strategic_advice:
When you finish writing a sentence, do a quick **'capital and spelling check'**:
- Check that all names and the word **'I'** are capitalised.
- Read your sentence **one word at a time** to catch any spelling slips or extra words.

patterns.name: Cloze Passage Clues
patterns.what: Occasionally picks a word for the blank that makes sense on its own but doesn't quite fit the grammar or exact meaning of the whole sentence.

specific_examples.whatWentWrong: EG chose 'through' instead of 'because', which doesn't match the grammar of the phrase 'of a partnership'.
EG used 'number' instead of 'risk', which doesn't quite fit the phrase 'increasing the risk of food poisoning'.
EG wrote 'early' instead of 'earlier', missing the clue that the sentence is comparing two things.

patterns.strategic_advice:
When you see **'Fill in each blank'**, treat the words immediately before and after the blank as your biggest clues:
- Look out for prepositions like **'of'** or **'to'** right after the blank.
- Read the **whole sentence** with your word to check if it sounds natural and fits the exact meaning.

patterns.name: Tricky Grammar Words
patterns.what: Sometimes gets tripped up by tricky grammar rules in multiple-choice questions, especially when choosing between 'who' and 'whom'.

specific_examples.whatWentWrong: EG chose 'whom' instead of 'who' when referring to the man performing the action.
EG chose 'who' instead of 'whom' after the preposition 'to'.
EG picked the simple past tense 'gave' instead of the perfect participle 'having given' to show an action that was completely finished.

patterns.strategic_advice:
When you see **'who'** and **'whom'** in the options, try the **'he/him' trick**:
- If you can replace the word with 'he', use **who**.
- If you can replace it with 'him' (especially after words like 'to' or 'with'), use **whom**.

patterns.name: Rewriting Sentences
patterns.what: Tends to miss a grammar rule or leave out a key phrase when rewriting sentences, such as shifting tenses in reported speech.

specific_examples.whatWentWrong: EG kept the present tense 'has completed' instead of shifting it back to the past tense 'had completed'.
EG left out the reported version of 'Don't worry', which meant the full meaning wasn't carried over.
EG missed out the verb 'is' after 'Reading novels', which made the sentence incomplete.

patterns.strategic_advice:
When you see prompts like **'wanted to know'** or **'asked'**, remember to **step back in time**:
- Change present tense verbs to **past tense** (e.g., 'has' becomes 'had').
- Double-check that **every part** of the original quote is included in your new sentence.
